raw_consumer_statement:
I am filing this complaint regarding Barclays handling of unauthorized transactions and subsequent billing inaccuracies on my account. 

Between XX/XX/XXXX and XX/XX/XXXX, approximately {$6200.00} in transactions were made that I did not authorize. I reported these charges immediately on XX/XX/XXXX. 

Barclays initially credited the full amount of the disputed transactions. However, they later reversed approximately {$4100.00} of those credits, claiming the charges were valid. 

After this reversal, the account balance became inconsistent and increased beyond the original disputed amount. At one point, the balance exceeded {$9000.00}, which is significantly higher than the original {$6200.00} in disputed transactions. 

Following multiple calls and escalations, Barclays adjusted the balance again, reducing it to approximately {$7800.00}. I made a payment of {$410.00} solely to avoid credit damage while this dispute remains unresolved, bringing the balance to approximately {$7400.00}. 

Throughout this process, Barclays has been inconsistent in both the amounts applied to the account and in its explanation of the investigation. 

Barclays has denied my fraud claim based primarily on a single alleged SMS verification related to one transaction. However, they have refused to provide any supporting documentation, including : The content of the SMS message Timestamped authentication records Device/IP verification logs Any merchant-provided evidence I have repeatedly requested this information and have not received it. 

Additionally, I was verbally informed by a Barclays representative in XXXX XXXX that the case had been reclassified from fraud to a billing dispute with a 30-day resolution timeframe. However, I have received no written confirmation of this change and no evidence that a proper dispute investigation was conducted. 

I have provided Barclays with documentation from the merchant ( XXXX XXXX XXXX  ) showing that during the same timeframe : My account was flagged for shared device usage Multiple accounts were using the same payment methods There were repeated failed deposit attempts This indicates irregular and potentially unauthorized activity, which Barclays has not meaningfully addressed. 

Despite the dispute remaining unresolved, Barclays continues to assess interest and has reported or is preparing to report the balance, causing potential harm to my credit. 

I believe Barclays has failed to conduct a reasonable investigation and has also failed to maintain accurate billing on the account.
